1	# Entire Token Cost Benchmark Tests
2	
3	Benchmark harness for comparing agent runs with and without Entire CLI
4	assistance.
5	
6	The core question is:
7	
8	> Does using Entire CLI reduce model token cost to reach an accepted result,
9	> compared with using the same coding agent without Entire?
10	
11	## Method
12	
13	The harness runs paired trials:
14	
15	1. Pick a real task from a target repository.
16	2. Start both arms from the same commit.
17	3. Run a fresh `codex exec --json` worker session for each arm.
18	4. Run configured checks such as `npm run build`.
19	5. Run a blind reviewer model that sees the task, final answer/diff, and checks,
20	   but not which arm produced the work.
21	6. Save verdicts, token usage, check logs, and summarized results.
22	
23	The main metric is worker token cost to accepted output. Reviewer token usage is
24	recorded separately as experiment overhead.
25	
26	## Arms
27	
28	- `baseline`: Entire is disabled and `entire` commands are blocked.
29	- `entire`: Entire is enabled and the worker is encouraged to use targeted
30	  checkpoint/explain commands first, with search as an optional secondary
31	  source when it returns useful repo-scoped results.
32	
33	## Current Pilot
34	
35	Saved pilot runs:
36	
37	- [summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T08-15-33-275Z-4528a2/summary.md)
38	- [summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T08-15-33-275Z-4528a2/summary.json)
39	- [corrected summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/corrected-pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T14-54-19-606Z-0bac51/summary.md)
40	- [corrected summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/corrected-pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T14-54-19-606Z-0bac51/summary.json)
41	- [improved-harness summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/improved-harness-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T18-11-07-499Z-3584e2/summary.md)
42	- [improved-harness summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/improved-harness-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T18-11-07-499Z-3584e2/summary.json)
43	- [3-replicate summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.md)
44	- [3-replicate summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.json)
45	- [Level 2 ablation summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.md)
46	- [Level 2 ablation summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.json)
47	
48	Important caveats:
49	
50	- The first pilot was launched before setup rewrote benchmark clone remotes back
51	  to the real GitHub `origin` URL. Treat it as a loop-mechanics pilot, not a
52	  final estimate.
53	- A follow-up diagnostic found that auth is healthy, but repo-scoped
54	  `entire checkpoint search` currently returns zero Planetfall results or times
55	  out. Local `entire checkpoint explain --json --limit 100` works in both the
56	  source repo and benchmark clone. See the
57	  [diagnostic summary](benchmarks/token-cost/results/diagnostic-2026-06-23-entire-search-vs-explain/summary.md).
58	- After changing the Entire prompt to start from local checkpoint metadata, the
59	  improved-harness rerun showed 31.10% fewer Entire worker tokens
60	  (1,313,069 vs 1,905,634) and a higher pass rate (3/3 vs 2/3).
61	- In synthetic benchmark branches, broad checkpoint listing may return `[]`;
62	  the harness now tells the Entire arm to discover checkpoint IDs from
63	  `Entire-Checkpoint:` git trailers and then run targeted
64	  `entire checkpoint explain <id> --json --no-pager`.
65	- The 3-replicate rerun showed high variance and reversed the single-run
66	  headline: both modes passed all 9 trials, but Entire used 27.11% more worker
67	  tokens overall. Level 2 evolution slightly favored Entire; progression and
68	  favicon favored baseline.
69	- The first Level 2 ablation showed all modes passing, with baseline cheapest
70	  and `entire-search-first` the cheapest Entire strategy in that replicate.
71	  This suggests command strategy changes behavior, but variance remains large.
72	- The gated Entire run avoided broad search/list calls and used only targeted
73	  checkpoint probes, but still used 34.77% more worker tokens than baseline
74	  across the three tasks. See the
75	  [gated summary](benchmarks/token-cost/results/gated-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T21-11-46-371Z-3c924f/summary.md).
76	
77	## Ablation Modes
78	
79	The harness supports `--modes` to compare command strategies:
80	
81	- `baseline`: blocks Entire and uses normal repo inspection.
82	- `entire`: current balanced Entire prompt.
83	- `entire-search-first`: starts with `entire checkpoint search --json`.
84	- `entire-list-first`: starts with broad checkpoint metadata.
85	- `entire-trailer-first`: starts from `Entire-Checkpoint:` git trailers, then
86	  targeted `entire checkpoint explain`.
87	- `entire-gated`: caps Entire to a small evidence probe and falls back quickly.
88	
89	## Usage
90	
91	From a local checkout:
92	
93	```bash
94	node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs setup
95	node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --dry-run
96	node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --execute
97	```
98	
99	Common overrides:
100	
101	```bash
102	node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run \
103	  --execute \
104	  --replicates 3 \
105	  --max-revisions 2 \
106	  --worker-model gpt-5.5 \
107	  --reviewer-model gpt-5.4
108	```
109	
110	Ablation example:
111	
112	```bash
113	node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run \
114	  --execute \
115	  --limit 1 \
116	  --modes baseline,entire-search-first,entire-list-first,entire-trailer-first,entire-gated
117	```
118	
119	The script currently defaults to the local Planetfall repository paths used for
120	the initial experiment. Override `--source`, `--baseline-root`, `--entire-root`,
121	and `--runs-root` to run it somewhere else.
122	
123	## Privacy
124	
125	Raw child-agent JSONL logs are intentionally ignored. They can contain full
126	agent transcripts, local paths, and repository context. Commit summarized
127	results deliberately.

file.content:
# Entire Token Cost Benchmark Tests

Benchmark harness for comparing agent runs with and without Entire CLI
assistance.

The core question is:

> Does using Entire CLI reduce model token cost to reach an accepted result,
> compared with using the same coding agent without Entire?

## Method

The harness runs paired trials:

1. Pick a real task from a target repository.
2. Start both arms from the same commit.
3. Run a fresh `codex exec --json` worker session for each arm.
4. Run configured checks such as `npm run build`.
5. Run a blind reviewer model that sees the task, final answer/diff, and checks,
   but not which arm produced the work.
6. Save verdicts, token usage, check logs, and summarized results.

The main metric is worker token cost to accepted output. Reviewer token usage is
recorded separately as experiment overhead.

## Arms

- `baseline`: Entire is disabled and `entire` commands are blocked.
- `entire`: Entire is enabled and the worker is encouraged to use targeted
  checkpoint/explain commands first, with search as an optional secondary
  source when it returns useful repo-scoped results.

## Current Pilot

Saved pilot runs:

- [summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T08-15-33-275Z-4528a2/summary.md)
- [summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T08-15-33-275Z-4528a2/summary.json)
- [corrected summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/corrected-pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T14-54-19-606Z-0bac51/summary.md)
- [corrected summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/corrected-pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T14-54-19-606Z-0bac51/summary.json)
- [improved-harness summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/improved-harness-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T18-11-07-499Z-3584e2/summary.md)
- [improved-harness summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/improved-harness-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T18-11-07-499Z-3584e2/summary.json)
- [3-replicate summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.md)
- [3-replicate summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.json)
- [Level 2 ablation summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.md)
- [Level 2 ablation summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.json)

Important caveats:

- The first pilot was launched before setup rewrote benchmark clone remotes back
  to the real GitHub `origin` URL. Treat it as a loop-mechanics pilot, not a
  final estimate.
- A follow-up diagnostic found that auth is healthy, but repo-scoped
  `entire checkpoint search` currently returns zero Planetfall results or times
  out. Local `entire checkpoint explain --json --limit 100` works in both the
  source repo and benchmark clone. See the
  [diagnostic summary](benchmarks/token-cost/results/diagnostic-2026-06-23-entire-search-vs-explain/summary.md).
- After changing the Entire prompt to start from local checkpoint metadata, the
  improved-harness rerun showed 31.10% fewer Entire worker tokens
  (1,313,069 vs 1,905,634) and a higher pass rate (3/3 vs 2/3).
- In synthetic benchmark branches, broad checkpoint listing may return `[]`;
  the harness now tells the Entire arm to discover checkpoint IDs from
  `Entire-Checkpoint:` git trailers and then run targeted
  `entire checkpoint explain <id> --json --no-pager`.
- The 3-replicate rerun showed high variance and reversed the single-run
  headline: both modes passed all 9 trials, but Entire used 27.11% more worker
  tokens overall. Level 2 evolution slightly favored Entire; progression and
  favicon favored baseline.
- The first Level 2 ablation showed all modes passing, with baseline cheapest
  and `entire-search-first` the cheapest Entire strategy in that replicate.
  This suggests command strategy changes behavior, but variance remains large.
- The gated Entire run avoided broad search/list calls and used only targeted
  checkpoint probes, but still used 34.77% more worker tokens than baseline
  across the three tasks. See the
  [gated summary](benchmarks/token-cost/results/gated-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T21-11-46-371Z-3c924f/summary.md).

## Ablation Modes

The harness supports `--modes` to compare command strategies:

- `baseline`: blocks Entire and uses normal repo inspection.
- `entire`: current balanced Entire prompt.
- `entire-search-first`: starts with `entire checkpoint search --json`.
- `entire-list-first`: starts with broad checkpoint metadata.
- `entire-trailer-first`: starts from `Entire-Checkpoint:` git trailers, then
  targeted `entire checkpoint explain`.
- `entire-gated`: caps Entire to a small evidence probe and falls back quickly.

## Usage

From a local checkout:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs setup
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --dry-run
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --execute
```

Common overrides:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run \
  --execute \
  --replicates 3 \
  --max-revisions 2 \
  --worker-model gpt-5.5 \
  --reviewer-model gpt-5.4
```

Ablation example:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run \
  --execute \
  --limit 1 \
  --modes baseline,entire-search-first,entire-list-first,entire-trailer-first,entire-gated
```

The script currently defaults to the local Planetfall repository paths used for
the initial experiment. Override `--source`, `--baseline-root`, `--entire-root`,
and `--runs-root` to run it somewhere else.

## Privacy

Raw child-agent JSONL logs are intentionally ignored. They can contain full
agent transcripts, local paths, and repository context. Commit summarized
results deliberately.

# Research Checkpoint - 2026-06-23

This note captures the working context from the Codex conversation that created
and reran the Entire token-cost benchmark. It exists because the benchmark repo
has an Entire checkpoint trailer for the session, but a plain git-tracked note
is easier for a future reviewer to inspect without depending on transcript
replay.

## Current Question

We are testing whether an agent that uses Entire can spend fewer total worker
tokens than an otherwise similar agent using normal CLI/source/git inspection.

The first serious answer is: not yet on this task set. Entire can improve the
quality of historical grounding, but the current worker prompts often make the
agent pay for both historical context and normal source investigation.

## What Is Pushed

- Benchmark repo: `blackgirlbytes/entire-token-costs-benchmark-tests`.
- Main benchmark script: `scripts/token-benchmark.mjs`.
- Task manifest: `benchmarks/token-cost/tasks.json`.
- Current attached Codex session: `019ef35b-2300-75e0-98d6-8e4dce14050f`.
- Current checkpoint trailer used on recent commits: `Entire-Checkpoint:
  a71156299398`.

## Key Results

1. Three-replicate run:
   - Baseline: 9/9 pass, 4,701,593 worker tokens.
   - Entire: 9/9 pass, 5,976,238 worker tokens.
   - Entire used 27.11% more worker tokens overall.
   - Level 2 evolution slightly favored Entire; progression/navigation and
     favicon favored baseline.
   - Result file:
     `benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.md`.

2. Level 2 strategy ablation:
   - All modes passed.
   - Baseline was cheapest in that replicate.
   - `entire-search-first` was the cheapest Entire strategy, but still above
     baseline for that specific ablation.
   - Result file:
     `benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.md`.

3. Gated Entire run:
   - Baseline: 3/3 pass, 1,063,608 worker tokens.
   - `entire-gated`: 3/3 pass, 1,433,363 worker tokens.
   - `entire-gated` used 34.77% more worker tokens overall.
   - Result file:
     `benchmarks/token-cost/results/gated-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T21-11-46-371Z-3c924f/summary.md`.

## What We Learned

The gated prompt changed behavior in the intended direction:

- No broad `entire checkpoint search` calls.
- No broad checkpoint-list exploration.
- Each gated worker used only 2-4 Entire commands.
- Each gated worker used one git-trailer probe.
- Each gated worker used 1-2 targeted `entire checkpoint explain` calls.

But gated Entire still cost more because the worker also:

- Loaded the local `using-entire` skill file.
- Read checkpoint/history evidence.
- Then performed substantial conventional source/git inspection anyway.

So the current benchmark may be measuring duplicated context-gathering cost
more than the theoretical value of Entire.

## Reviewer Questions

A different reviewer model should audit the methodology before more expensive
replicates:

- Are these tasks too easy for baseline, making historical context mostly
  overhead?
- Are the prompts causing Entire users to over-document provenance compared
  with baseline users?
- Is loading the local `using-entire` skill inside every child trial fair, or
  should the benchmark test a mode that provides the minimal command recipe in
  the prompt and forbids skill-file loading?
- Should the next arm precompute checkpoint snippets outside the worker and
  inject only the relevant excerpts into the prompt?
- Should the metric separate gross tokens from billable tokens after cache
  reads, or continue comparing total worker tokens?

## Next Recommended Ablations

1. `entire-no-skill`: allow `entire status`, one trailer lookup, and targeted
   explains, but explicitly forbid reading `.codex/skills/using-entire/SKILL.md`
   or other local skill files inside child trials.

2. `entire-precomputed-context`: have the harness run the Entire lookup before
   the worker starts, then pass a small checkpoint excerpt bundle to both arms
   or to a dedicated Entire arm. This tests whether the historical evidence
   itself helps without charging every worker for discovery mechanics.

3. Task-fit split: add tasks where history should matter more than source
   inspection, such as "why did this design change", "restore removed behavior",
   or "continue a prior partial implementation from checkpoint intent".

## Current Interpretation

Entire is not automatically token-saving. It becomes expensive when the agent
uses it as an additional research layer instead of a replacement for source
rediscovery. The promising direction is to make Entire usage narrower,
precomputed, or task-gated so it supplies only the context that baseline would
otherwise spend many source/git reads reconstructing.

content.text:
I've read the whole thing — the harness, the tasks, all the result summaries, and your research note. First: this is a genuinely well-built harness (blind reviewer, randomized arm order, paired trials from the same base commit, token accounting from the JSONL). Your research note already smells the problem ("measuring duplicated context-gathering cost more than the theoretical value of Entire"). Let me sharpen *why* that's happening, because it's the crux.

## The core problem: you've built a scenario where Entire can only add cost, never save it

Entire's token value proposition is **substitution** — recovering context from a checkpoint *instead of* re-deriving it from source/git. Your benchmark is structured so Entire is **additive** instead:

- Both arms start cold from the same commit, in a **single fresh session**.
- The baseline agent reads source + git and answers. That's cheap, and it's complete.
- The Entire agent does that **same** source/git reading **plus** `entire status` + checkpoint explain/list/trailer probes + loads the `using-entire` skill file.

There is no rediscovery tax for Entire to eliminate, because nothing was discovered in a *prior* session that the baseline is being forced to reconstruct. So Entire is pure overhead on top of work the baseline already does for free. The benchmark is almost guaranteed to show Entire losing — and it does (-27%, -34%), exactly as the mechanism predicts.

Compounding it: 2 of your 3 tasks are "explain the current code" questions answerable straight from `src/` + `README`. History is overhead for those. The favicon task has zero historical dependency at all. **None of your tasks make rediscovery from source expensive**, which is the only condition under which a memory/checkpoint tool wins on tokens.

## To show savings, the task must make source-rediscovery expensive and checkpoint-retrieval a *substitute*

The savings only appear when retrieving from a checkpoint is **cheaper than and replaces** re-deriving from source. That needs one of:

1. **Cross-session continuation.** Session A does partial work and checkpoints. Session B continues it. Baseline B has only the code and must reconstruct intent/approach/dead-ends; Entire B reads the checkpoint. This is Entire's actual home-field workflow and your harness doesn't test it at all — every trial is a one-shot.
2. **"Why" / removed-behavior questions.** "Why was X removed?" / "restore the behavior that used to do Y." The answer is *no longer in the source* — baseline must crawl full diff history (expensive, token-heavy), Entire reads one checkpoint with the intent. Source rediscovery is genuinely costly here.
3. **Large/deep codebases** where baseline's source exploration is itself the dominant token sink. Planetfall is tiny, so baseline can just read everything cheaply — the worst case for showing retrieval savings.

## Even before the scenario, the measurement can't carry a conclusion yet

A few things mean your current numbers can't support *any* headline, in either direction:

- **The aggregate % is dominated by outliers.** You sum raw tokens across 3 heterogeneous tasks, then divide. Look at `level-progression-navigation` entire: rep1 = 1,556,851 vs rep2 = 376,737 — a 4× swing within the *same* arm/task. One bad trial sets your headline. With 3 tasks × 3 reps you have no power to see anything smaller than the noise. Report **per-task paired deltas** and a distribution (geometric mean of per-task ratios, or a paired test), never a summed grand total.
- **Raw `total_tokens` is the wrong cost unit.** You take the last cumulative `token_count` event, which lumps cached input, fresh input, output, and reasoning together at equal weight. The Entire arm inflates *input* tokens (skill file + checkpoint JSON), but cached input is ~10× cheaper to bill. You're penalizing Entire on a metric that doesn't match the actual dollar cost. Weight by price with the cache discount, or at minimum break out fresh-input / cached-input / output / reasoning separately. You flagged this yourself ("gross vs billable") — it's not a footnote, it can flip the result.
- **You're benchmarking a degraded Entire.** Your own notes say `entire checkpoint search` returns empty or times out in this repo, so you fell back to `checkpoint explain --limit 100` and git-trailer crawls — the *most* verbose retrieval paths. The one feature most likely to save tokens (targeted semantic retrieval) isn't working in your harness. Fix that before drawing conclusions, or you're measuring Entire-with-its-best-tool-broken.
- **The skill-file tax is a fixed cost mixed into a marginal measurement.** Loading `using-entire/SKILL.md` in every child trial is a constant per-session onboarding cost. Separate "fixed cost of having Entire in the session" from "marginal cost/savings of using it for this task" — otherwise short tasks always look bad and you can't tell which effect you're seeing.

## What I'd actually do

I'd reframe and re-scope rather than keep tuning prompts (the ablations are rearranging deck chairs on the additive-cost problem):

1. **Add a continuation task type.** Programmatically: run a "session A" that does partial work + checkpoints, snapshot, then both arms run "session B" continuing it. This is the test Entire is built to win and you have zero of them.
2. **Add "why/removed-behavior" tasks** where the answer is in history, not current source.
3. **Switch the metric to billable cost** with cache-aware weighting, reported as **per-task paired ratios** with variance, not a summed percentage.
4. **Fix or exclude `entire checkpoint search`** so you're testing the real tool.
5. **Separate fixed vs marginal cost** (report skill-load/`entire status` overhead apart from retrieval).
6. Bump replicates (5–10) once a single task type shows a stable per-task delta — no point spending tokens on more arms while the scenario itself can't produce signal.

So: you're not measuring the metric wrong, you're measuring the right metric on a scenario that structurally can't show the effect. "Worker tokens to accepted output" is fine; "single cold session answering questions about current code in a tiny repo, with search broken and raw tokens as the unit" is the part that guarantees Entire looks like overhead.

Want me to sketch the harness changes for the **cross-session continuation** task type (the highest-leverage fix), or rework the summarizer to do **cache-weighted, per-task paired** reporting first?

147		if err != nil && opts.Review {
148			cmd.SilenceUsage = true
149			fmt.Fprintln(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), err.Error())
150			return NewSilentError(err)
151		}
152		return err
153	}
154	
155	// attachStepCount returns the displayed "steps" count for an attached session:
156	// the number of user prompts (turns) in the attached transcript, as counted by
157	// extractTranscriptMetadata. Floored at 1 so it never renders as "0 steps" for an
158	// empty/unparseable transcript. SaveStepCount stays 0 (no SaveStep ran), keeping
159	// the combined-attribution gate conservative for this fallback session.
160	func attachStepCount(turnCount int) int {
161		return max(turnCount, 1)
162	}
163	
164	// attachPrompts returns the prompts recorded on an attached checkpoint. Attach
165	// records only the first user prompt (used for the display title); the full
166	// per-turn list isn't reconstructed for post-hoc imports.
167	func attachPrompts(meta transcriptMetadata) []string {
168		if meta.FirstPrompt == "" {
169			return nil
170		}
171		return []string{meta.FirstPrompt}
172	}
173	
174	func runAttach(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer, sessionID string, agentName types.AgentName, opts attachOptions) error {
175		// Initialize structured logger so logging.Warn/Info write to .entire/logs/ not stderr.
176		if err := logging.Init(ctx, sessionID); err != nil {
177			// Init failed — logging will use stderr fallback, non-fatal.
178			_ = err
179		}
180		// Flush the 8KB buffered log writer on exit. Without this, any
181		// Warn/Info calls during attach (including the overwrite tripwire)
182		// get silently dropped when the process exits, matching the pattern
183		// already used by resume/clean/reset/rewind/explain.
184		defer logging.Close()
185	
186		logCtx := logging.WithComponent(ctx, "attach")
187	
188		// Open repository once — shared across all operations.
189		repo, err := openRepository(ctx)
190		if err != nil {
191			return err
192		}
193		defer func() {
194			if closeErr := repo.Close(); closeErr != nil {
195				logging.Warn(logCtx, "failed to close repository", slog.String("error", closeErr.Error()))
196			}
197		}()
198	
199		existingState, err := validateAttachPreconditions(ctx, repo, sessionID)
200		if err != nil {
201			return err
202		}
203	
204		headCommit, err := getHeadCommit(repo)
205		if err != nil {
206			return err
207		}
208	
209		// If session already has a checkpoint, just offer to link it.
210		if existingState != nil && !existingState.LastCheckpointID.IsEmpty() {
211			// Review-upgrade isn't supported yet: the existing checkpoint's
212			// metadata tree would need to be rewritten with Kind/ReviewSkills/
213			// ReviewPrompt set, and a new commit pushed onto entire/checkpoints/v1.
214			// Error out with a concrete message rather than silently linking the
215			// checkpoint without the review metadata.
216			if opts.Review {
217				return fmt.Errorf(
218					"session %s already has checkpoint %s; rewriting an existing checkpoint as a review is not supported yet",
219					sessionID, existingState.LastCheckpointID.String(),
220				)
221			}
222			cpID := existingState.LastCheckpointID.String()
223			fmt.Fprintf(w, "Session %s already has checkpoint %s\n", sessionID, cpID)
224			if err := promptAmendCommit(logCtx, w, headCommit, cpID, opts.Force); err != nil {
225				logging.Warn(logCtx, "failed to amend commit", "error", err)
226				fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nCopy to your commit message to attach:\n\n  Entire-Checkpoint: %s\n", cpID)
227			}
228			return nil
229		}
230	
231		// Resolve agent and transcript path.
232		ag, transcriptPath, err := resolveAgentAndTranscript(logCtx, w, sessionID, agentName, existingState)
233		if err != nil {
234			return err
235		}
236	
237		var reviewSkills []string
238		if opts.Review {
239			reviewSkills = resolveReviewSkills(opts.ReviewSkillsOverride)
240		}
241	
242		transcriptData, err := ag.ReadTranscript(transcriptPath)
243		if err != nil {
244			return fmt.Errorf("failed to read transcript: %w", err)
245		}
246	
247		// Normalize Gemini transcripts for storage.
248		storedTranscript := transcriptData
249		if ag.Type() == agent.AgentTypeGemini {
250			if normalized, normErr := geminicli.NormalizeTranscript(transcriptData); normErr == nil {
251				storedTranscript = normalized
252			} else {
253				logging.Warn(logCtx, "failed to normalize Gemini transcript, storing raw", "error", normErr)
254			}
255		}
256	
257		meta := extractTranscriptMetadata(transcriptData)
258	
259		// Determine checkpoint ID: reuse from HEAD if one exists, otherwise generate new.
260		checkpointID, isExistingCheckpoint := resolveCheckpointID(headCommit)
261	
262		// If HEAD references an existing checkpoint, make sure we have it locally
263		// before writing — otherwise we'd create a fresh session 0 under the same
264		// ID and overwrite the original on push.
265		refs := opts.committedRefs(ctx)
266		refreshedRepo, err := ensureCheckpointAvailable(ctx, logCtx, repo, refs, checkpointID, isExistingCheckpoint)
267		if refreshedRepo != nil && refreshedRepo != repo {
268			oldRepo := repo
269			repo = refreshedRepo
270			if closeErr := oldRepo.Close(); closeErr != nil {
271				logging.Warn(logCtx, "failed to close stale repository handle after checkpoint refresh",
272					slog.String("error", closeErr.Error()))
273			}
274		}
275		if err != nil {
276			return err
277		}
278	
279		store, err := openAttachStore(ctx, repo, refs)
280		if err != nil {
281			return err
282		}
283	
284		// Defense-in-depth guard: the earlier existingState.LastCheckpointID
285		// check only fires when the session's state file records its
286		// checkpoint. A session already stored in the HEAD checkpoint but
287		// whose state is missing/stale (state file deleted, never written,
288		// condensed without LastCheckpointID update, or pulled from a remote
289		// that wasn't reflected locally) would bypass that guard.
290		// findSessionIndex matches by SessionID — without this check, a
291		// review-attach on such a session silently overwrites the existing
292		// session's metadata in the checkpoint.
293		if opts.Review && isExistingCheckpoint {
294			exists, readErr := checkpointHasSessionMetadata(ctx, repo, refs, checkpointID, sessionID)
295			if readErr != nil {
296				return fmt.Errorf("failed to check checkpoint %s for session %s: %w", checkpointID.String(), sessionID, readErr)
297			}
298			if exists {
299				return fmt.Errorf(
300					"session %s is already recorded in checkpoint %s; rewriting an existing checkpoint as a review is not supported yet",
301					sessionID, checkpointID.String(),
302				)
303			}
304		}
305	
306		author, err := GetGitAuthor(ctx)
307		if err != nil {
308			return fmt.Errorf("failed to get git author: %w", err)
309		}
310	
311		tokenUsage := agent.CalculateTokenUsage(logCtx, ag, transcriptData, 0, "")
312	
313		_, redactSpan := perf.Start(ctx, "redact_transcript")
314		redactedTranscript, redactErr := redact.JSONLBytes(storedTranscript)
315		redactSpan.End()
316		if redactErr != nil {
317			return fmt.Errorf("failed to redact transcript: %w", redactErr)
318		}
319	
320		writeOpts := cpkg.WriteCommittedOptions{
321			CheckpointID:     checkpointID,
322			SessionID:        sessionID,
323			Strategy:         strategy.StrategyNameManualCommit,
324			Transcript:       redactedTranscript,
325			Prompts:          attachPrompts(meta),
326			CheckpointsCount: attachStepCount(meta.TurnCount),
327			AuthorName:       author.Name,
328			AuthorEmail:      author.Email,
329			Agent:            ag.Type(),
330			Model:            meta.Model,
331			TokenUsage:       tokenUsage,
332		}
333		if opts.Review {
334			writeOpts.Kind = string(session.KindAgentReview)
335			writeOpts.ReviewSkills = reviewSkills
336			writeOpts.ReviewPrompt = reviewPromptForAttach(meta, opts)
337			writeOpts.HasReview = true
338		}
339	
340		if err := store.Write(ctx, cpkg.WriteSession(writeOpts)); err != nil {
341			return fmt.Errorf("failed to write checkpoint: %w", err)
342		}
343	
344		// Create or update session state.
345		if err := saveAttachSessionState(logCtx, repo, existingState, sessionID, ag.Type(), transcriptPath, checkpointID, meta, tokenUsage, opts, reviewSkills); err != nil {
346			logging.Warn(logCtx, "failed to save session state", "error", err)
347		}
348	
349		fmt.Fprintf(w, "Attached session %s\n", sessionID)
350		if isExistingCheckpoint {
351			fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Added to existing checkpoint %s\n", checkpointID)
352			return nil
353		}
354	
355		fmt.Fprintf(w, "  Created checkpoint %s\n", checkpointID)
356		cpIDStr := checkpointID.String()
357		if err := promptAmendCommit(logCtx, w, headCommit, cpIDStr, opts.Force); err != nil {
358			logging.Warn(logCtx, "failed to amend commit", "error", err)
359			fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nCopy to your commit message to attach:\n\n  Entire-Checkpoint: %s\n", cpIDStr)
360		}
361	
362		return nil
363	}
364	
365	// checkpointHasSessionMetadata reports whether sessionID has existing metadata
366	// at Primary. Reads target Primary directly, not refs.Read, because this guard
367	// must reflect what the next write would target.
368	func checkpointHasSessionMetadata(ctx context.Context, repo *git.Repository, refs cpkg.CommittedRefs, checkpointID id.CheckpointID, sessionID string) (bool, error) {
369		store, err := openAttachStore(ctx, repo, refs.PrimaryAsRead())
370		if err != nil {
371			return false, err
372		}
373		summary, err := store.ReadCommitted(ctx, checkpointID)
374		if err != nil {
375			return false, fmt.Errorf("read checkpoint summary: %w", err)
376		}
377		if summary == nil {
378			return false, nil
379		}
380		for i := range summary.Sessions {
381			metadata, err := store.ReadSessionMetadata(ctx, checkpointID, i)
382			if err != nil {
383				return false, fmt.Errorf("read session %d metadata: %w", i, err)
384			}
385			if metadata != nil && metadata.SessionID == sessionID {
386				return true, nil
387			}
388		}
389		return false, nil
390	}

392	// getHeadCommit returns the HEAD commit object.
393	func getHeadCommit(repo *git.Repository) (*object.Commit, error) {
394		headRef, err := repo.Head()
395		if err != nil {
396			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get HEAD: %w", err)
397		}
398		commit, err := repo.CommitObject(headRef.Hash())
399		if err != nil {
400			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get HEAD commit: %w", err)
401		}
402		return commit, nil
403	}
404	
405	// ensureCheckpointAvailable makes sure the checkpoint referenced by HEAD is
406	// present locally before the attach writes to it. Without this guard, attach
407	// would create a fresh session 0 under the same ID and overwrite the original
408	// session data on push.
409	//
410	// Only the local branch counts — remote-tracking presence is not enough.
411	// If only the remote-tracking ref exists, a subsequent WriteCommitted creates
412	// a brand-new orphan local branch with an empty tree, which would clobber
413	// the remote on push.
414	//
415	// Fast path: check local refs directly — no network. If missing, trigger the
416	// metadata fetch fallback chain used by `entire resume` (which advances the
417	// local ref on success) and re-check. Returns a possibly-freshly-opened repo
418	// handle so go-git sees any newly fetched packfiles.
419	func ensureCheckpointAvailable(ctx, logCtx context.Context, repo *git.Repository, refs cpkg.CommittedRefs, checkpointID id.CheckpointID, isExistingCheckpoint bool) (*git.Repository, error) {
420		if !isExistingCheckpoint {
421			return repo, nil
422		}
423	
424		present, readErr := checkpointPresentLocally(ctx, repo, refs, checkpointID)
425		if readErr != nil {
426			return repo, fmt.Errorf("failed to read checkpoint %s: %w", checkpointID, readErr)
427		}
428		if present {
429			return repo, nil
430		}
431	
432		// Missing locally — try to refresh, then re-check. Use the same fetch
433		// chain `entire resume` uses for the primary metadata ref.
434		freshRepo, fetchErr := refreshCheckpointRefs(ctx)
435		if fetchErr != nil {
436			logging.Warn(logCtx, "failed to refresh metadata branch before attach; proceeding with local state",
437				slog.String("error", fetchErr.Error()))
438		} else {
439			repo = freshRepo
440			present, readErr = checkpointPresentLocally(ctx, repo, refs, checkpointID)
441			if readErr != nil {
442				return repo, fmt.Errorf("failed to read checkpoint %s after refresh: %w", checkpointID, readErr)
443			}
444			if present {
445				return repo, nil
446			}
447		}
448	
449		branchDescription := "entire/checkpoints/v1 branch"
450		return repo, fmt.Errorf(
451			"checkpoint %s referenced by HEAD is missing from the local %s after a refresh attempt. Creating a fresh checkpoint here would overwrite the original session data on push. Run:\n\n    %s\n\nthen re-run attach. If the colleague who made this commit hasn't pushed their checkpoint metadata yet, ask them to do so first",
452			checkpointID.String(), branchDescription, suggestCheckpointFetchCommand(logCtx),
453		)
454	}
455	
456	// refreshCheckpointRefs runs the resume-equivalent fetch chain for the v1
457	// metadata branch. Returns a freshly-opened repo so go-git sees any
458	// newly-fetched packfiles and ref updates.
459	func refreshCheckpointRefs(ctx context.Context) (*git.Repository, error) {
460		_, repo, err := getMetadataTree(ctx)
461		return repo, err
462	}
463	
464	// checkpointPresentLocally reports whether the checkpoint already exists at
465	// Primary locally. Reads target Primary directly, not refs.Read, because this
466	// asks what the next write would find, not what readers see. A missing local
467	// ref is reported as absent; the caller is responsible for any remote refresh.
468	func checkpointPresentLocally(ctx context.Context, repo *git.Repository, refs cpkg.CommittedRefs, checkpointID id.CheckpointID) (bool, error) {
469		if _, err := repo.Reference(refs.Primary, true); err != nil {
470			return false, nil //nolint:nilerr // Missing ref is the "absent" signal, not an error.
471		}
472		store, err := openAttachStore(ctx, repo, refs.PrimaryAsRead())
473		if err != nil {
474			return false, err
475		}
476		summary, err := store.ReadCommitted(ctx, checkpointID)
477		if err != nil {
478			return false, err //nolint:wrapcheck // Caller wraps with checkpoint ID context
479		}
480		return summary != nil, nil
481	}
482	
483	// suggestCheckpointFetchCommand returns a git fetch command the user can
484	// paste to pull the missing v1 metadata branch.
485	func suggestCheckpointFetchCommand(ctx context.Context) string {
486		ref := "entire/checkpoints/v1:entire/checkpoints/v1"
487		if remote.Configured(ctx) {
488			if url, err := remote.FetchURL(ctx); err == nil && url != "" {
489				return fmt.Sprintf("git fetch %s %s", url, ref)
490			}
491		}
492		return "git fetch origin " + ref
493	}
494	
495	func resolveCheckpointID(headCommit *object.Commit) (id.CheckpointID, bool) {
496		existing := trailers.ParseAllCheckpoints(headCommit.Message)
497		if len(existing) > 0 {
498			return existing[len(existing)-1], true
499		}
500	
501		cpID, err := id.Generate()
502		if err != nil {
503			// Generation only fails if crypto/rand fails — extremely unlikely.
504			// Fall back to empty which will cause WriteCommitted to fail with a clear error.
505			return id.EmptyCheckpointID, false
506		}
507		return cpID, false
508	}
509	
510	// saveAttachSessionState creates or updates the session state file for the attached session.
511	// If existingState is non-nil, it is updated in place (avoids a redundant disk load).
512	// reviewSkills is the resolved skills list when opts.Review is true; ignored otherwise.
513	func saveAttachSessionState(ctx context.Context, repo *git.Repository, existingState *session.State, sessionID string, agentType types.AgentType, transcriptPath string, checkpointID id.CheckpointID, meta transcriptMetadata, tokenUsage *agent.TokenUsage, opts attachOptions, reviewSkills []string) error {
514		stateStore, err := session.NewStateStore(ctx)
515		if err != nil {
516			return fmt.Errorf("failed to open session store: %w", err)
517		}
518	
519		now := time.Now()
520		state := existingState
521		if state == nil {
522			state = &session.State{
523				SessionID: sessionID,
524				StartedAt: now,
525			}
526		}
527	
528		// Populate BaseCommit from HEAD if not already set, so the session becomes
529		// active and future commits in the same session receive Entire-Checkpoint trailers.
530		if state.BaseCommit == "" {
531			if head, headErr := repo.Head(); headErr == nil {
532				headHash := head.Hash().String()
533				state.BaseCommit = headHash
534				state.AttributionBaseCommit = headHash
535			}
536		}
537	
538		state.CLIVersion = versioninfo.Version
539		state.AttachedManually = true
540		state.AgentType = agentType
541		state.TranscriptPath = transcriptPath
542		state.LastCheckpointID = checkpointID
543		// Only transition to Ended if the session is not already active — avoid
544		// breaking an ongoing session whose BaseCommit has just been restored above.
545		if !state.Phase.IsActive() {
546			state.Phase = session.PhaseEnded
547		}
548		state.LastInteractionTime = &now
549		if meta.TurnCount > 0 {
550			state.SessionTurnCount = meta.TurnCount
551		}
552		if meta.Model != "" {
553			state.ModelName = meta.Model
554		}
555		if meta.FirstPrompt != "" {
556			state.LastPrompt = meta.FirstPrompt
557		}
558		if tokenUsage != nil {
559			state.TokenUsage = tokenUsage
560		}
561		if opts.Review {
562			state.Kind = session.KindAgentReview
563			state.ReviewSkills = reviewSkills
564			state.ReviewPrompt = reviewPromptForAttach(meta, opts)
565		}
566	
567		if err := stateStore.Save(ctx, state); err != nil {
568			return fmt.Errorf("failed to save session state: %w", err)
569		}
570		return nil
571	}
572	
573	func reviewPromptForAttach(meta transcriptMetadata, opts attachOptions) string {
574		if opts.ReviewPromptOverride != "" {
575			return opts.ReviewPromptOverride
576		}
577		return meta.FirstPrompt
578	}

580	// validateAttachPreconditions checks session ID format and git repo state.
581	// Returns the existing session state if the session is already tracked (nil if new).
582	func validateAttachPreconditions(ctx context.Context, repo *git.Repository, sessionID string) (*session.State, error) {
583		if err := validation.ValidateSessionID(sessionID); err != nil {
584			return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid session ID: %w", err)
585		}
586	
587		if strategy.IsEmptyRepository(repo) {
588			return nil, errors.New("repository has no commits yet — make an initial commit before running attach")
589		}
590	
591		store, err := session.NewStateStore(ctx)
592		if err != nil {
593			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to open session store: %w", err)
594		}
595		existing, err := store.Load(ctx, sessionID)
596		if err != nil {
597			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to check existing session: %w", err)
598		}
599	
600		return existing, nil
601	}
602	
603	// resolveAgentAndTranscript resolves the agent and transcript path.
604	// For existing sessions, resolves the agent from session state's AgentType.
605	// For new sessions, uses the --agent flag with auto-detection fallback.
606	func resolveAgentAndTranscript(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer, sessionID string, agentName types.AgentName, existingState *session.State) (agent.Agent, string, error) {
607		ag, err := resolveAgent(existingState, agentName)
608		if err != nil {
609			return nil, "", err
610		}
611	
612		transcriptPath, err := resolveAndValidateTranscript(ctx, sessionID, ag)
613		if err != nil {
614			// Auto-detect: try all other agents.
615			detectedAg, detectedPath, detectErr := detectAgentByTranscript(ctx, sessionID, agentName)
616			if detectErr != nil {
617				return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("%w (also tried auto-detecting other agents: %w)", err, detectErr)
618			}
619			ag = detectedAg
620			transcriptPath = detectedPath
621			logging.Info(ctx, "auto-detected agent from transcript", "agent", ag.Name())
622			fmt.Fprintf(w, "Auto-detected agent: %s\n", ag.Name())
623		}
624	
625		return ag, transcriptPath, nil
626	}
627	
628	// resolveAgent resolves the agent to use. For existing sessions with an AgentType,
629	// uses agent.GetByAgentType. Otherwise falls back to the --agent flag.
630	func resolveAgent(existingState *session.State, agentName types.AgentName) (agent.Agent, error) {
631		if existingState != nil && existingState.AgentType != "" {
632			ag, err := agent.GetByAgentType(existingState.AgentType)
633			if err == nil {
634				return ag, nil
635			}
636			// Fall through to flag-based resolution.
637		}
638		ag, err := agent.Get(agentName)
639		if err != nil {
640			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent %q not available: %w", agentName, err)
641		}
642		return ag, nil
643	}
644	
645	// resolveAndValidateTranscript finds the transcript file for a session, searching alternative
646	// project directories if needed.
647	func resolveAndValidateTranscript(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, ag agent.Agent) (string, error) {
648		transcriptPath, err := resolveTranscriptPath(ctx, sessionID, ag)
649		if err != nil {
650			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve transcript path: %w", err)
651		}
652		// Only call PrepareTranscript when the file already exists — it flushes
653		// in-progress writes, but can't conjure a file that was never started.
654		// This avoids agents like Cursor polling for 3s on non-existent files
655		// during auto-detection.
656		if _, statErr := os.Stat(transcriptPath); statErr == nil {
657			if preparer, ok := agent.AsTranscriptPreparer(ag); ok {
658				if prepErr := preparer.PrepareTranscript(ctx, transcriptPath); prepErr != nil {
659					logging.Debug(ctx, "PrepareTranscript failed (best-effort)", "error", prepErr)
660				}
661			}
662			return transcriptPath, nil
663		}
664		found, searchErr := searchTranscriptInProjectDirs(sessionID, ag)
665		if searchErr == nil {
666			logging.Info(ctx, "found transcript in alternative project directory", "path", found)
667			return found, nil
668		}
669		logging.Debug(ctx, "fallback transcript search failed", "error", searchErr)
670		return "", fmt.Errorf("transcript not found for agent %q with session %s; is the session ID correct?", ag.Name(), sessionID)
671	}
672	
673	// detectAgentByTranscript tries all registered agents (except skip) to find one whose
674	// transcript resolution succeeds for the given session ID.
675	func detectAgentByTranscript(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, skip types.AgentName) (agent.Agent, string, error) {
676		for _, name := range agent.List() {
677			if name == skip {
678				continue
679			}
680			ag, err := agent.Get(name)
681			if err != nil {
682				continue
683			}
684			path, resolveErr := resolveAndValidateTranscript(ctx, sessionID, ag)
685			if resolveErr != nil {
686				logging.Debug(ctx, "auto-detect: agent did not match", "agent", string(name), "error", resolveErr)
687				continue
688			}
689			return ag, path, nil
690		}
691		return nil, "", errors.New("transcript not found for any registered agent")
692	}
693	
694	// promptAmendCommit shows the last commit and asks whether to amend it with the checkpoint trailer.
695	// When force is true, it amends without prompting.
696	func promptAmendCommit(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer, headCommit *object.Commit, checkpointIDStr string, force bool) error {
697		shortHash := headCommit.Hash.String()[:7]
698		subject := strings.SplitN(headCommit.Message, "\n", 2)[0]
699	
700		// Skip amending if this exact checkpoint ID is already in the commit.
701		for _, existing := range trailers.ParseAllCheckpoints(headCommit.Message) {
702			if existing.String() == checkpointIDStr {
703				fmt.Fprintf(w, "Commit %s already has Entire-Checkpoint: %s\n", shortHash, checkpointIDStr)
704				return nil
705			}
706		}
707	
708		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nLast commit: %s %s\n", shortHash, subject)
709	
710		amend := true
711		if !force {
712			if !interactive.CanPromptInteractively() {
713				// Non-interactive: can't prompt, print trailer for manual use.

# Entire Token Cost Benchmark Tests

Benchmark harness for comparing agent runs with and without Entire CLI
assistance.

The core question is:

> Does using Entire CLI reduce model token cost to reach an accepted result,
> compared with using the same coding agent without Entire?

## Method

The harness runs paired trials:

1. Pick a real task from a target repository.
2. Start both arms from the same commit.
3. Run a fresh `codex exec --json` worker session for each arm.
4. Run configured checks such as `npm run build`.
5. Run a blind reviewer model that sees the task, final answer/diff, and checks,
   but not which arm produced the work.
6. Save verdicts, token usage, check logs, and summarized results.

The main metric is worker token cost to accepted output. Reviewer token usage is
recorded separately as experiment overhead.

## Arms

- `baseline`: Entire is disabled and `entire` commands are blocked.
- `entire`: Entire is enabled and the worker is encouraged to use targeted
  checkpoint/explain commands first, with search as an optional secondary
  source when it returns useful repo-scoped results.

## Current Pilot

Saved pilot runs:

- [summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T08-15-33-275Z-4528a2/summary.md)
- [summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T08-15-33-275Z-4528a2/summary.json)
- [corrected summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/corrected-pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T14-54-19-606Z-0bac51/summary.md)
- [corrected summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/corrected-pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T14-54-19-606Z-0bac51/summary.json)
- [improved-harness summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/improved-harness-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T18-11-07-499Z-3584e2/summary.md)
- [improved-harness summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/improved-harness-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T18-11-07-499Z-3584e2/summary.json)
- [3-replicate summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.md)
- [3-replicate summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.json)
- [Level 2 ablation summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.md)
- [Level 2 ablation summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.json)

Important caveats:

- The first pilot was launched before setup rewrote benchmark clone remotes back
  to the real GitHub `origin` URL. Treat it as a loop-mechanics pilot, not a
  final estimate.
- A follow-up diagnostic found that auth is healthy, but repo-scoped
  `entire checkpoint search` currently returns zero Planetfall results or times
  out. Local `entire checkpoint explain --json --limit 100` works in both the
  source repo and benchmark clone. See the
  [diagnostic summary](benchmarks/token-cost/results/diagnostic-2026-06-23-entire-search-vs-explain/summary.md).
- After changing the Entire prompt to start from local checkpoint metadata, the
  improved-harness rerun showed 31.10% fewer Entire worker tokens
  (1,313,069 vs 1,905,634) and a higher pass rate (3/3 vs 2/3).
- In synthetic benchmark branches, broad checkpoint listing may return `[]`;
  the harness now tells the Entire arm to discover checkpoint IDs from
  `Entire-Checkpoint:` git trailers and then run targeted
  `entire checkpoint explain <id> --json --no-pager`.
- The 3-replicate rerun showed high variance and reversed the single-run
  headline: both modes passed all 9 trials, but Entire used 27.11% more worker
  tokens overall. Level 2 evolution slightly favored Entire; progression and
  favicon favored baseline.
- The first Level 2 ablation showed all modes passing, with baseline cheapest
  and `entire-search-first` the cheapest Entire strategy in that replicate.
  This suggests command strategy changes behavior, but variance remains large.
- The gated Entire run avoided broad search/list calls and used only targeted
  checkpoint probes, but still used 34.77% more worker tokens than baseline
  across the three tasks. See the
  [gated summary](benchmarks/token-cost/results/gated-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T21-11-46-371Z-3c924f/summary.md).

## Ablation Modes

The harness supports `--modes` to compare command strategies:

- `baseline`: blocks Entire and uses normal repo inspection.
- `entire`: current balanced Entire prompt.
- `entire-search-first`: starts with `entire checkpoint search --json`.
- `entire-list-first`: starts with broad checkpoint metadata.
- `entire-trailer-first`: starts from `Entire-Checkpoint:` git trailers, then
  targeted `entire checkpoint explain`.
- `entire-gated`: caps Entire to a small evidence probe and falls back quickly.

## Usage

From a local checkout:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs setup
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --dry-run
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --execute
```

Common overrides:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run \
  --execute \
  --replicates 3 \
  --max-revisions 2 \
  --worker-model gpt-5.5 \
  --reviewer-model gpt-5.4
```

Ablation example:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run \
  --execute \
  --limit 1 \
  --modes baseline,entire-search-first,entire-list-first,entire-trailer-first,entire-gated
```

The script currently defaults to the local Planetfall repository paths used for
the initial experiment. Override `--source`, `--baseline-root`, `--entire-root`,
and `--runs-root` to run it somewhere else.

## Privacy

Raw child-agent JSONL logs are intentionally ignored. They can contain full
agent transcripts, local paths, and repository context. Commit summarized
results deliberately.

input.old_string: ## Privacy
input.new_string:
## Metrics and methodology

The summarizer (`summarize` / end of `run`) reports cost-weighted, paired,
per-token-class results rather than a single summed token percentage:

- **Billable cost, not raw tokens.** Raw `total_tokens` mixes cheap cache-read
  input, full-price fresh input, and output at equal weight. The Entire arm
  inflates *input* tokens (skill file + checkpoint JSON), but cache-read input
  is ~10x cheaper to bill, so a token-count delta does not match the dollar
  delta. Costs use a per-model pricing table — override the placeholders with
  `--pricing <path-or-json>` or `BENCH_PRICING_JSON` before trusting the numbers.
- **Per-token-class breakdown.** Each mode reports fresh-input / cached-input /
  output / reasoning separately so you can see *where* an arm spends.
- **Per-task paired deltas, aggregated by geometric mean.** A summed grand-total
  percentage is dominated by whichever single task is most expensive — that is
  how the earlier headline flipped between runs. Each (task, rep) Entire trial is
  paired with its baseline trial; the per-pair cost ratio is aggregated with a
  geometric mean (and median). `cost_ratio < 1` / positive `cost_savings%` means
  Entire is cheaper. Per-task rows show which tasks help and which hurt.
- **Fixed vs marginal Entire cost.** Every trial reports
  `entire_marginal~` — an approximate count of tokens the worker ingested from
  Entire-specific command output and skill-file reads (heuristic JSONL scan).
  This separates Entire-attributable spend from the shared task investigation
  both arms do. The `entire-overhead-probe` task isolates the *fixed* overhead
  (skill load + `entire status`) on a question that needs no history.

## Continuation tasks

A continuation task declares a `seed` (session A). The harness runs the seed
prompt in the Entire-enabled root, commits the partial work so the commit
carries an `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer, publishes the seed commit + checkpoint
metadata, and then both arms continue from that seed commit — only the Entire
arm can read the prior session's checkpoint.

```bash
# Seed once, capture the base SHA:
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs seed --task continue-partial-feature --execute

# Then run both arms from the seed:
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --execute --include-drafts \
  --manifest benchmarks/token-cost/tasks-history.json \
  --task continue-partial-feature --seed-base <sha>
```

History-dependent and continuation task templates live in
`benchmarks/token-cost/tasks-history.json` and are marked `"draft": true` (fill
in the repo-specific `<BEHAVIOR>` / `<FEATURE>` / `base` placeholders, then run
with `--include-drafts`).

## Privacy

# Entire Token Cost Benchmark Tests

Benchmark harness for comparing agent runs with and without Entire CLI
assistance.

The core question is:

> Does using Entire CLI reduce model token cost to reach an accepted result,
> compared with using the same coding agent without Entire?

## Method

The harness runs paired trials:

1. Pick a real task from a target repository.
2. Start both arms from the same commit.
3. Run a fresh `codex exec --json` worker session for each arm.
4. Run configured checks such as `npm run build`.
5. Run a blind reviewer model that sees the task, final answer/diff, and checks,
   but not which arm produced the work.
6. Save verdicts, token usage, check logs, and summarized results.

The main metric is worker **billable cost** to accepted output (not raw token
count — see [Metrics and methodology](#metrics-and-methodology)). Reviewer token
usage is recorded separately as experiment overhead.

### What this benchmark can and cannot show

Entire's token value comes from **substitution**: recovering context from a
checkpoint *instead of* re-deriving it from source/git. A single cold session
that answers a question about current code gives Entire nothing to substitute —
it only adds a retrieval layer on top of investigation the baseline already does
cheaply, so Entire looks like pure overhead. Savings appear only when
source-rediscovery is genuinely expensive and checkpoint-retrieval replaces it:

- **Cross-session continuation** — session A checkpoints partial work; session B
  continues it. Baseline B reconstructs intent from code; Entire B reads the
  checkpoint. See [Continuation tasks](#continuation-tasks).
- **"Why / removed-behavior" questions** — the answer is no longer in source, so
  baseline must crawl full diff history while Entire reads one checkpoint. See
  `benchmarks/token-cost/tasks-history.json`.

The default `tasks.json` set is the *worst case* for showing savings; treat its
results as a methodology sanity check, not the headline.

## Arms

- `baseline`: Entire is disabled and `entire` commands are blocked.
- `entire`: Entire is enabled and the worker is encouraged to use targeted
  checkpoint/explain commands first, with search as an optional secondary
  source when it returns useful repo-scoped results.

## Current Pilot

Saved pilot runs:

- [summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T08-15-33-275Z-4528a2/summary.md)
- [summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T08-15-33-275Z-4528a2/summary.json)
- [corrected summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/corrected-pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T14-54-19-606Z-0bac51/summary.md)
- [corrected summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/corrected-pilot-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T14-54-19-606Z-0bac51/summary.json)
- [improved-harness summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/improved-harness-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T18-11-07-499Z-3584e2/summary.md)
- [improved-harness summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/improved-harness-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T18-11-07-499Z-3584e2/summary.json)
- [3-replicate summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.md)
- [3-replicate summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.json)
- [Level 2 ablation summary.md](benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.md)
- [Level 2 ablation summary.json](benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.json)

Important caveats:

- The first pilot was launched before setup rewrote benchmark clone remotes back
  to the real GitHub `origin` URL. Treat it as a loop-mechanics pilot, not a
  final estimate.
- A follow-up diagnostic found that auth is healthy, but repo-scoped
  `entire checkpoint search` currently returns zero Planetfall results or times
  out. Local `entire checkpoint explain --json --limit 100` works in both the
  source repo and benchmark clone. See the
  [diagnostic summary](benchmarks/token-cost/results/diagnostic-2026-06-23-entire-search-vs-explain/summary.md).
- After changing the Entire prompt to start from local checkpoint metadata, the
  improved-harness rerun showed 31.10% fewer Entire worker tokens
  (1,313,069 vs 1,905,634) and a higher pass rate (3/3 vs 2/3).
- In synthetic benchmark branches, broad checkpoint listing may return `[]`;
  the harness now tells the Entire arm to discover checkpoint IDs from
  `Entire-Checkpoint:` git trailers and then run targeted
  `entire checkpoint explain <id> --json --no-pager`.
- The 3-replicate rerun showed high variance and reversed the single-run
  headline: both modes passed all 9 trials, but Entire used 27.11% more worker
  tokens overall. Level 2 evolution slightly favored Entire; progression and
  favicon favored baseline.
- The first Level 2 ablation showed all modes passing, with baseline cheapest
  and `entire-search-first` the cheapest Entire strategy in that replicate.
  This suggests command strategy changes behavior, but variance remains large.
- The gated Entire run avoided broad search/list calls and used only targeted
  checkpoint probes, but still used 34.77% more worker tokens than baseline
  across the three tasks. See the
  [gated summary](benchmarks/token-cost/results/gated-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T21-11-46-371Z-3c924f/summary.md).

## Ablation Modes

The harness supports `--modes` to compare command strategies:

- `baseline`: blocks Entire and uses normal repo inspection.
- `entire`: current balanced Entire prompt.
- `entire-search-first`: starts with `entire checkpoint search --json`.
- `entire-list-first`: starts with broad checkpoint metadata.
- `entire-trailer-first`: starts from `Entire-Checkpoint:` git trailers, then
  targeted `entire checkpoint explain`.
- `entire-gated`: caps Entire to a small evidence probe and falls back quickly.

## Usage

From a local checkout:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs setup
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --dry-run
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --execute
```

Common overrides:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run \
  --execute \
  --replicates 3 \
  --max-revisions 2 \
  --worker-model gpt-5.5 \
  --reviewer-model gpt-5.4
```

Ablation example:

```bash
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run \
  --execute \
  --limit 1 \
  --modes baseline,entire-search-first,entire-list-first,entire-trailer-first,entire-gated
```

The script currently defaults to the local Planetfall repository paths used for
the initial experiment. Override `--source`, `--baseline-root`, `--entire-root`,
and `--runs-root` to run it somewhere else.

## Metrics and methodology

The summarizer (`summarize` / end of `run`) reports cost-weighted, paired,
per-token-class results rather than a single summed token percentage:

- **Billable cost, not raw tokens.** Raw `total_tokens` mixes cheap cache-read
  input, full-price fresh input, and output at equal weight. The Entire arm
  inflates *input* tokens (skill file + checkpoint JSON), but cache-read input
  is ~10x cheaper to bill, so a token-count delta does not match the dollar
  delta. Costs use a per-model pricing table — override the placeholders with
  `--pricing <path-or-json>` or `BENCH_PRICING_JSON` before trusting the numbers.
- **Per-token-class breakdown.** Each mode reports fresh-input / cached-input /
  output / reasoning separately so you can see *where* an arm spends.
- **Per-task paired deltas, aggregated by geometric mean.** A summed grand-total
  percentage is dominated by whichever single task is most expensive — that is
  how the earlier headline flipped between runs. Each (task, rep) Entire trial is
  paired with its baseline trial; the per-pair cost ratio is aggregated with a
  geometric mean (and median). `cost_ratio < 1` / positive `cost_savings%` means
  Entire is cheaper. Per-task rows show which tasks help and which hurt.
- **Fixed vs marginal Entire cost.** Every trial reports
  `entire_marginal~` — an approximate count of tokens the worker ingested from
  Entire-specific command output and skill-file reads (heuristic JSONL scan).
  This separates Entire-attributable spend from the shared task investigation
  both arms do. The `entire-overhead-probe` task isolates the *fixed* overhead
  (skill load + `entire status`) on a question that needs no history.

## Continuation tasks

A continuation task declares a `seed` (session A). The harness runs the seed
prompt in the Entire-enabled root, commits the partial work so the commit
carries an `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer, publishes the seed commit + checkpoint
metadata, and then both arms continue from that seed commit — only the Entire
arm can read the prior session's checkpoint.

```bash
# Seed once, capture the base SHA:
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs seed --task continue-partial-feature --execute

# Then run both arms from the seed:
node scripts/token-benchmark.mjs run --execute --include-drafts \
  --manifest benchmarks/token-cost/tasks-history.json \
  --task continue-partial-feature --seed-base <sha>
```

History-dependent and continuation task templates live in
`benchmarks/token-cost/tasks-history.json` and are marked `"draft": true` (fill
in the repo-specific `<BEHAVIOR>` / `<FEATURE>` / `base` placeholders, then run
with `--include-drafts`).

## Privacy

Raw child-agent JSONL logs are intentionally ignored. They can contain full
agent transcripts, local paths, and repository context. Commit summarized
results deliberately.

## Addendum — methodology rework (this branch)

A reviewer pass concluded the original benchmark measured the right metric on
the wrong scenario: every trial was a single cold session, so Entire was
structurally additive (it could only add a retrieval layer, never substitute for
rediscovery the baseline already does cheaply). This branch makes four changes:

1. **Cost, not raw tokens.** The summarizer now reports billable USD cost via a

   over-penalized the Entire arm, whose extra input is largely cached.

2. **Per-task paired deltas, geometric mean.** Replaced the summed-total
   `savingsPct` (dominated by the single most expensive task — the reason the
   headline flipped between runs) with per-(task,rep) baseline-vs-mode ratios
   aggregated by geometric mean + median, plus per-task rows.

3. **Fixed vs marginal Entire cost.** Each trial now reports an approximate
   `entire_marginal~` token count (heuristic JSONL scan of Entire command output
   + skill-file reads), and a new `entire-overhead-probe` task isolates the fixed
   skill-load/`entire status` overhead on a no-history question.

4. **History-dependent + continuation scenarios.** Added
   `tasks-history.json` (why/removed-behavior templates) and a `seed` phase +
   `continuation` task kind so session A checkpoints partial work and both arms
   continue from it — the workflow where Entire substitution should actually pay
   off. Templates are `draft:true` pending repo-specific fill-in.

Open items the next run must resolve before any headline: fix or exclude
`entire checkpoint search` (still empty/timeout here, so the cheapest retrieval
path is untested), set real model prices, fill in the history/continuation
templates, and raise replicates once a single task type shows a stable per-task
delta.

# Research Checkpoint - 2026-06-23

This note captures the working context from the Codex conversation that created
and reran the Entire token-cost benchmark. It exists because the benchmark repo
has an Entire checkpoint trailer for the session, but a plain git-tracked note
is easier for a future reviewer to inspect without depending on transcript
replay.

## Current Question

We are testing whether an agent that uses Entire can spend fewer total worker
tokens than an otherwise similar agent using normal CLI/source/git inspection.

The first serious answer is: not yet on this task set. Entire can improve the
quality of historical grounding, but the current worker prompts often make the
agent pay for both historical context and normal source investigation.

## What Is Pushed

- Benchmark repo: `blackgirlbytes/entire-token-costs-benchmark-tests`.
- Main benchmark script: `scripts/token-benchmark.mjs`.
- Task manifest: `benchmarks/token-cost/tasks.json`.
- Current attached Codex session: `019ef35b-2300-75e0-98d6-8e4dce14050f`.
- Current checkpoint trailer used on recent commits: `Entire-Checkpoint:
  a71156299398`.

## Key Results

1. Three-replicate run:
   - Baseline: 9/9 pass, 4,701,593 worker tokens.
   - Entire: 9/9 pass, 5,976,238 worker tokens.
   - Entire used 27.11% more worker tokens overall.
   - Level 2 evolution slightly favored Entire; progression/navigation and
     favicon favored baseline.
   - Result file:
     `benchmarks/token-cost/results/replicates-3-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T19-42-56-421Z-109d16/summary.md`.

2. Level 2 strategy ablation:
   - All modes passed.
   - Baseline was cheapest in that replicate.
   - `entire-search-first` was the cheapest Entire strategy, but still above
     baseline for that specific ablation.
   - Result file:
     `benchmarks/token-cost/results/ablation-level-2-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T20-47-04-297Z-0bfc68/summary.md`.

3. Gated Entire run:
   - Baseline: 3/3 pass, 1,063,608 worker tokens.
   - `entire-gated`: 3/3 pass, 1,433,363 worker tokens.
   - `entire-gated` used 34.77% more worker tokens overall.
   - Result file:
     `benchmarks/token-cost/results/gated-2026-06-23-run-2026-06-23T21-11-46-371Z-3c924f/summary.md`.

## What We Learned

The gated prompt changed behavior in the intended direction:

- No broad `entire checkpoint search` calls.
- No broad checkpoint-list exploration.
- Each gated worker used only 2-4 Entire commands.
- Each gated worker used one git-trailer probe.
- Each gated worker used 1-2 targeted `entire checkpoint explain` calls.

But gated Entire still cost more because the worker also:

- Loaded the local `using-entire` skill file.
- Read checkpoint/history evidence.
- Then performed substantial conventional source/git inspection anyway.

So the current benchmark may be measuring duplicated context-gathering cost
more than the theoretical value of Entire.

## Reviewer Questions

A different reviewer model should audit the methodology before more expensive
replicates:

- Are these tasks too easy for baseline, making historical context mostly
  overhead?
- Are the prompts causing Entire users to over-document provenance compared
  with baseline users?
- Is loading the local `using-entire` skill inside every child trial fair, or
  should the benchmark test a mode that provides the minimal command recipe in
  the prompt and forbids skill-file loading?
- Should the next arm precompute checkpoint snippets outside the worker and
  inject only the relevant excerpts into the prompt?
- Should the metric separate gross tokens from billable tokens after cache
  reads, or continue comparing total worker tokens?

## Next Recommended Ablations

1. `entire-no-skill`: allow `entire status`, one trailer lookup, and targeted
   explains, but explicitly forbid reading `.codex/skills/using-entire/SKILL.md`
   or other local skill files inside child trials.

2. `entire-precomputed-context`: have the harness run the Entire lookup before
   the worker starts, then pass a small checkpoint excerpt bundle to both arms
   or to a dedicated Entire arm. This tests whether the historical evidence
   itself helps without charging every worker for discovery mechanics.

3. Task-fit split: add tasks where history should matter more than source
   inspection, such as "why did this design change", "restore removed behavior",
   or "continue a prior partial implementation from checkpoint intent".

## Current Interpretation

Entire is not automatically token-saving. It becomes expensive when the agent
uses it as an additional research layer instead of a replacement for source
rediscovery. The promising direction is to make Entire usage narrower,
precomputed, or task-gated so it supplies only the context that baseline would
otherwise spend many source/git reads reconstructing.
